---
title: "AD698 - Applied Generative AI"
subtitle: "Technology Stack Preparation & Deep Learning Foundations"
number-sections: true
format: 
    html:
        code-fold: true
    docx: default
date: "2026-01-01"
date-modified: today
date-format: long
categories: ['M0:', 'Wrapup']
description: "Module 0 wrap-up for Technology Stack Preparation & Deep Learning Foundations."
---


# What Did We Learn?

In this module, we established the **computational and theoretical foundation** for all subsequent topics in generative AI.

We learned that modern AI systems are built on **large-scale numerical computation**, where GPUs enable efficient execution of tensor operations. Using environments like Google Colab, we can seamlessly experiment with these systems in a reproducible way.

We explored how **neural networks learn from data** by:

* transforming inputs through layered computations
* measuring error using loss functions
* updating parameters using gradients

This introduced the core principle that learning is fundamentally an **optimization process over high-dimensional parameter spaces**.

Finally, we examined the **sequence learning problem**, which highlights why text and language are fundamentally different from static datasets. Unlike traditional machine learning tasks, language requires models to capture **order, context, and dependencies across time**, motivating the need for specialized architectures.

# Conceptual Bridge to Next Module

We now move from:

> **Static computation and learning**
> to
> **Dynamic sequence modeling with memory**

The next module will address:

* How models retain information across time
* Why standard neural networks fail on long sequences
* How recurrent architectures (RNNs, LSTMs, GRUs) overcome these challenges
